---
title: "LRPITT02: ITT analysis of taught expressive vocabulary, block 1 (TE)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPITT02 is the intention-to-treat (ITT) analysis for taught expressive vocabulary, block 1 (`b1extau`). The
estimand is the total effect of randomised group assignment $G$ on the post-score
$TE_{\text{post}}$, using the RCT phase only (time 1 $\rightarrow$ time 2).
This model supersedes LRP74 (which conditioned on the standardised-vocabulary cross-baseline).

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**. The own baseline $\gamma_{\text{own}}$
and linear age $\gamma_A$ are **precision terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ but are
not what licenses the causal interpretation, and no cross-baseline conditioning is
used. Attendance / sessions are never conditioned on (a DAG collider).
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(TE_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i},
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $TE_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 24$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.
HSGPs on age and own baseline are off by default (the 2026-04-18 sensitivity
verdict, `notes/202604181445-lrp52-gp-sensitivity.md`).

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=32%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=32%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=32%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and equal-tailed central interval (coverage `ci_prob`) of $\tau$ on the logit scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: average marginal effect on the probability scale (risk difference), averaged over the fitted sample per posterior draw.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0) = P(\text{intervention helps})$.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
